posts.cooked: Hi, I saw LBA 2 island viewer. But it has no export. How can I export the island as a 3d model? Or tell me where I can find a description of the island format and objects on it.

Hey, given that Mills hasnt updated us with his progress in the old thread you also replied to, it looks like he didnt get around to writing an exporter, and I guess neither did anyone else.

magicse:

Hi, I saw LBA 2 island viewer. But it has no export. How can I export the island as a 3d model? Or tell me where I can find a description of the island format and objects on it.

The updated link that Lupin mentioned in that thread would be LBA2:Citabau.ile - LBA File Information
And the source code that xesf mentioned seems to now be at lba2remake/src/game/scenery/island at master LBALab/lba2remake GitHub

Well then, good luck, and keep us up to date with your progress

bloodhound:

magicse:

some test

Yeah would be cool to remake the islands in that style! (Though its still a bit too regular in some places in terms of color and shape). What did you use to create this?

Its a pity creating an island exporter is so hard. I think for a programmer not familiar with this it would take at least a month, to learn about the export format, how to stitch the individual parts together into an island model, how to add textures and colors, etc.

bloodhound:

What did you use to create this?

3d Max

Island extractor https://fex.net/s/e6kv2dn

exampe of use

LBA2IslandExtractor.exe d:\twinsen\

Extrated files willl be in folder Out

[d:\twinsen] - path to *.ILE, *.OBL and RESS.HQR files from origina disk

posts.cooked: Wow, how did you manage to come up with something in only a week? Really cool! I dont have a Windows/Intel PC available today, but in a few days Ill definitely check it out! Whats your next goal with this?

Yes sure please check it.
There, the height of the island is not exported correctly now it turns out to be twice as high as necessary

I just checked it out, awesome how this is getting along! I always thought the houses were part of the island files. Without the houses I didnt even recognize your screenshot as Citadel Island :D

For exposure I think if your tool is open source you can add it to LBA Lab GitHub, or if its not, then you could ask elmuerte to add it to the MBNs download section. Of course it would be great if it converted all the island models, not just Citadel Island.

Without the houses I didnt even recognize your screenshot as Citadel Island :D

For exposure I think if your tool is open source you can add it to LBA Lab GitHub, or if its not, then you could ask elmuerte to add it to the MBNs download section.

Still in the coding process. There are bugs and some fixes.

Of course it would be great if it converted all the island models, not just Citadel Island.

Yes, of course, I will complete the possibility of exporting any islands.

Now Im posting the export of the citadel island with the correct heights.

posts.cooked: So as of now youve used the layout info for the edges and vertices and then the textures and texture UVs to map the textures onto the faces?

Something like that

Island.jpg1465835 185 KB

Hey magicse, Reeks post today referencing an island exporter for LBA1 made me think about your project. Are you still working on open sourcing it? I think its a useful tool, and open sourcing it makes it easier to get it running on our more exotic operating systems.
I second the quest for open-sourcing it.
We could probably make a simplifies version of the lba2remake source and create an exporter. The code is all there to be used, just need to be stripped from the actual game.
